questions.question: What is N8N primarily used for?
Which trigger type starts a workflow when an external system sends data?
What data format do most N8N nodes use internally?
What is a 'node' in N8N?
options: A single step or action in a workflow

questions.question: Which node is used to make HTTP API calls in N8N?
What does the IF node do in N8N?
How do you handle errors gracefully in N8N workflows?
What does 'Split in Batches' node do?
options: Processes large data sets in smaller chunks

questions.question: What is the correct N8N expression syntax to access a JSON field from the previous node?
What does the 'Merge' node do in N8N?
options: Combines data streams from multiple branches into one

questions.question: Where are API credentials (API keys, OAuth tokens) stored securely in N8N?
What is the 'Code' node in N8N used for?
options: Writing and executing custom JavaScript logic inside a workflow

questions.question: Which N8N node would you use to pause execution until a specific time?
What is the main advantage of self-hosted N8N over N8N Cloud?
options: Full data privacy and control over infrastructure

questions.question: In N8N, what does 'pinning' data to a node allow you to do?
options: Test the workflow using fixed sample data without re-triggering
Copy the node to another workflow

questions.question: What is Make.com's basic building block called?
What is a 'scenario' in Make.com?
What does the Router module do?
options: Routes data to different paths based on conditions

questions.question: What is 'data mapping' in Make.com?
options: Connecting output from one module to input of another

questions.question: How often can Make.com scenarios be triggered?
options: On a schedule or triggered by webhooks

questions.question: What does a filter do in Make.com?
options: Only allows data to pass if conditions are met

questions.question: What is an 'iterator' in Make.com?
options: Processes each item in an array one by one

questions.question: What happens during 'rollback' in Make.com error handling?
options: Completed operations are undone to keep data consistent

questions.question: What is a 'bundle' in Make.com?
options: A single unit of data processed by a module in one execution

questions.question: How does Make.com count 'operations' for billing purposes?
options: Each module execution counts as one operation

questions.question: What does 'instant trigger' mean in Make.com?
options: Scenario starts immediately when a webhook event fires

questions.question: What is a Make.com 'Data Store' used for?
options: A built-in database to persist and retrieve data across scenario runs

questions.question: What does the 'Aggregator' module do in Make.com?
options: Combines multiple bundles from an iterator into a single bundle

questions.question: What is the 'Set Variable' module used for in Make.com?
options: Storing a value in memory to reuse later in the same scenario

questions.question: What does Make.com's 'HTTP' module allow you to do?
options: Make custom API requests to any URL not covered by built-in apps

questions.question: What is RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)?
options: Combining LLMs with external knowledge retrieval

questions.question: What is a vector database used for in AI systems?
What makes an AI system 'agentic'?
options: It can plan, use tools, and take actions autonomously

questions.question: What is LangChain primarily used for?
What is an 'embedding' in the context of RAG?
options: A numerical vector representation of text

questions.question: What is the main advantage of RAG over fine-tuning?
options: It allows real-time knowledge updates without retraining

questions.question: What does 'context window' mean in LLMs?
options: The maximum amount of text an LLM can process at once

questions.question: What is multi-agent orchestration?
options: Coordinating multiple AI agents to solve complex tasks together

questions.question: What is 'chunking' in a RAG pipeline?
options: Breaking source documents into smaller pieces before embedding

questions.question: In a ReAct agent pattern, what does 'ReAct' stand for?
options: Reasoning and Acting — the agent thinks step by step then acts

questions.question: What is 'hallucination' in the context of LLMs?
options: When the model generates confident but factually incorrect information

questions.question: What is 'temperature' in LLM inference settings?
options: A parameter controlling randomness — low=deterministic, high=creative

questions.question: What is semantic search?
options: Searching based on meaning and conceptual similarity using embeddings

questions.question: What is LlamaIndex primarily designed for?
options: Connecting LLMs to external data sources and building RAG applications

questions.question: What is 'model drift' and why does it matter for deployed AI agents?
options: Performance degradation over time as real-world data patterns change from training data

questions.question: What does AWS stand for?
What is serverless computing?
options: Computing where you don't manage or provision servers

questions.question: What is IAM in AWS?
What does 'auto-scaling' mean in cloud architecture?
options: Automatically adjusting resources based on demand

questions.question: What is a CDN (Content Delivery Network) used for?
options: Delivering content faster by serving from geographically closer servers

questions.question: Which cloud service model gives you the most infrastructure control?
What is AWS Lambda?
What is the purpose of an API Gateway in cloud architecture?
options: Act as entry point for APIs, handling auth, rate limiting, and routing

questions.question: What is an AWS VPC?
options: Virtual Private Cloud — an isolated network environment in AWS

questions.question: What does S3 stand for in AWS, and what is it used for?
options: Simple Storage Service — scalable object/file storage

questions.question: What is the difference between horizontal and vertical scaling?
options: Horizontal = faster CPU, Vertical = more RAM
Horizontal = add more instances, Vertical = increase single instance size

questions.question: What is a 'cold start' in serverless functions?
options: The startup delay when a function is invoked after being idle

questions.question: What is the cloud 'shared responsibility model'?
options: Customers share costs with the provider
Provider secures infrastructure; customer is responsible for data, app, and access
Both share equal security duties for all layers
Provider handles everything including customer data

questions.question: What is Kubernetes used for?
options: Orchestrating and managing containerised applications at scale

questions.question: What is a CI/CD pipeline in cloud DevOps?
options: Continuous Integration / Continuous Delivery — automating build, test, and deployment

questions.question: What does BGP stand for?
What is OSPF used for?
options: Interior gateway routing protocol for IP networks

questions.question: What is a VLAN?
What does Zero Trust security mean?
options: Never trust, always verify — every user and device must be authenticated

questions.question: What does subnetting allow you to do?
options: Divide a large network into smaller, manageable sub-networks

questions.question: What layer of the OSI model do switches primarily operate at?
What is SD-WAN?
What does an ACL (Access Control List) do?
options: Controls which traffic is allowed or denied in a network

questions.question: What is NAT (Network Address Translation)?
options: Maps private IP addresses to a public IP for internet communication

questions.question: What does CIDR notation '/24' mean for an IP subnet?
options: 24 bits used for the network, leaving 8 bits for hosts (256 addresses)

questions.question: What is ARP (Address Resolution Protocol) used for?
options: Mapping IP addresses to MAC addresses on a local network

questions.question: What is a 'trunk port' in network switching?
options: A port configured to carry traffic for multiple VLANs simultaneously
A port that blocks all traffic

questions.question: What is the key difference between TCP and UDP?
options: TCP is wireless, UDP is wired
TCP is reliable with error checking; UDP is fast with no guaranteed delivery

questions.question: What does DHCP do in a network?
options: Automatically assigns IP addresses, subnet masks, and gateway info to devices

questions.question: What is a 'default route' (0.0.0.0/0) used for?
options: The route of last resort — used when no specific route matches the destination

questions.question: What is the first step in an AI consulting engagement?
What does ROI stand for in AI projects?
Which processes are best candidates for AI automation?
What is 'change management' in AI adoption?
options: Managing people, processes and culture during AI transformation

questions.question: What is responsible AI?
options: Developing AI that is ethical, fair, transparent, and accountable

questions.question: What should an AI roadmap include?
options: Vision, milestones, resource requirements, and success metrics

questions.question: What is a key factor when evaluating AI vendors?
options: Integration capability, support, pricing, and data security

questions.question: How should AI solutions be presented to non-technical stakeholders?
options: Focus on business outcomes, ROI, and risk mitigation

questions.question: What is a 'Proof of Concept' (POC) in AI consulting?
options: A small-scale test to validate whether an AI solution is viable before full investment

questions.question: What is 'shadow AI' in an enterprise context?
options: Employees using AI tools without IT or management approval or oversight

questions.question: What is 'data governance' in AI projects?
options: Policies and processes ensuring data quality, security, access control, and compliance

questions.question: What KPI best measures success of an AI automation project?
options: Time saved, error rate reduction, cost per transaction, and throughput

questions.question: What is the 'build vs buy' decision in AI consulting?
options: Choosing between developing custom AI internally or purchasing an existing vendor solution

questions.question: What is 'model drift' and why does a consultant need to address it?
options: When an AI model's accuracy degrades over time as real data patterns shift from training data

questions.question: What does an AI readiness assessment evaluate?
options: Which AI tools to buy immediately
Data quality, infrastructure maturity, team skills, and cultural readiness for AI adoption
